name: Eternalism (philosophy of time)
snippet:
McTaggart viewed this as a contradiction in the concept of time itself, and concluded that reality is non-temporal. He called this concept the B - theory of time .

name: Time - Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy
snippet:
Nov 25, 2002 — McTaggart argued that there is in fact no such thing as time , and that the appearance of a temporal order to the world is a mere appearance.
McTaggart argues that the B series alone does not constitute a proper time series; the A series is essential to time. His reason for this is ...

name: Against the Block: A Realist's Rejection of the Block Universe
snippet: Cited by 1 — Eternalism, or the block universe theory , maintains that past, present, and future events all exist equally in a four-dimensional spacetime.
